app.header.tagline: Trash & trade filters for Pokémon GO. Protect what matters, sweep the rest.
app.step.where.label: Where do you play?
app.step.where.title: Where do you play?
app.step.where.hint: Pin your home location and discover which Pokémon are regional to you — we protect those automatically.
app.step.what.label: What do you want to keep?
app.step.what.title: What do you want to keep?
app.step.what.hint: Pick a preset as a starting point, then fine-tune — favorites, shinies, legendaries, and more.
app.step.have.label: What's in your collection?
app.step.have.title: What's in your collection?
app.step.have.hint: Your 4★ collection (hundos) — these widen the trash filter so you can clean up spares.
app.step.filter.label: Your filter
app.step.filter.title: Your filter
app.step.filter.hint: Done — copy the filter strings and paste them into PoGo's search box.
app.hundos.input_placeholder: Number or name — e.g. 1, Pikachu, Bulbasaur, 666
app.hundos.input_help: Accepts {numbers}, {english}, or {german} names. Separate multiple with comma, space, or semicolon.
app.top_attackers.empty: no top attackers yet — add the species you trust to bring to a raid
app.top_attackers.input_placeholder: Number or name — e.g. 150, Mewtwo, Garchomp, Lucario
app.top_attackers.input_help: Accepts {numbers}, {english}, or {german} names. Separate multiple with comma, space, or semicolon. These species get OR'd into every raid counter filter so they always show up alongside the type-resistor matches. Max Battles and Team Rocket fights skip the allowlist — different rosters / mechanics.
app.top_max_attackers.empty: no Max Battle attackers yet — add Dynamax / Gigantamax species you trust to bring
app.top_max_attackers.input_placeholder: Number or name — e.g. Gengar, Inteleon, Rillaboom
app.top_max_attackers.input_help: Accepts {numbers}, {english}, or {german} names. Separate multiple with comma, space, or semicolon. These species get OR'd into every Max Battle counter filter alongside the type-resistor matches. Raid filters use the regular top-attackers list — different roster.
app.collectibles.title: Your custom collectibles · added to trash protection
app.collectibles.description: Species you collect and never want to throw away — favorites, form collections, etc.
app.collectibles.input_placeholder: Number or name — e.g. Charizard, 6, Rayquaza
app.filterbox.select_all_hint: Tap to select all — then copy via the system menu
app.clauses.intro: Every clause in the final filter, with the reason it's there. Clauses are joined with & — every clause must match for a Pokémon to appear.
app.buddy_catch.section_subtitle: trashable Pokémon of the wish list species
app.filter.trash_hint: Mass-delete fodder. Inverts your protections so anything matching is safe to transfer.
app.filter.trade_hint: Daily-driver tradeable Pokémon. Untraded, non-shadow, non-lucky, IVs that beat your hundo by appraisal-bucket.
app.filter.sort_hint: Surfaces all members of your hundo families so you can pin them in your bag.
app.filter.prestaged_hint: Tagged trade pile ({tags}). Pre-curated so trade day is just hand it over.
app.filter.gift_hint: Send-worthy catches: shinies, legendaries, costumes, plus your home regionals (a friend abroad gets a non-local).
app.collapsible.raw_clauses: raw filter clauses · what comes from where
app.filter.shadow_cheap_hint: Common 1km-walk shadows still in default state (Frustration). Excludes shinies, favorites, tagged, plus any shadow you've Charge-TM'd off Frustration — those are real TM investments.
app.filter.shadow_safe_hint: Mass purify for take-over events. Default-state shadows only (Frustration not yet Charge-TM'd). Excludes legendaries, mythicals, ultra beasts, 4★, shinies, costumes, favorites, plus your raid-keeper species. TM'd shadows skip the filter automatically.
app.filter.shadow_hundo_candidates_hint: Default-state shadows with all three stats at IV bucket 3+ (≥13). Purify adds +2 (capped at 15), so every match becomes 15/15/15. Excludes Charge-TM'd shadows — purifying them would lose the TM and the +20% atk boost. Tap each in-game to confirm exact IVs before committing.
app.filter.shadow_frustration_hint: Take-over event TM target list: keeper-species shadows that still have Frustration. Charge TMs replace Frustration only during these events. Set an optional opt-in tag in Settings to flag extra Pokémon outside the keeper list.
app.filter.evo_swap_candy_hint: Trade-buddy purified-dex coordination. Shadows of species with a 400-candy single jump or cumulative ≥150 candy through final form (Magikarp, Wailmer, Swablu, Larvesta, Noibat, Stufful, Wimpod, Meltan, Toxel, Sinistea, Snom, Poltchageist, Roggenrola, Timburr, Karrablast, Shelmet, Phantump, Pumpkaboo, Type:Null, Poipole, Kubfu, Mankey, Teddiursa, Pawniard, Applin). Whole families included — Crypto-Gyarados surfaces too. Pick candidates, tag with #{tag} in-game, then ship via the Tagged-for-swap filter.
app.filter.evo_swap_item_hint: Trade-buddy purified-dex coordination. Shadows of species whose evolution requires a special item (Sinnoh / Unova / Sun Stone, King's Rock, Metal Coat, Dragon Scale, Up-Grade, Apple variants) or lure module (Magnetic / Mossy / Glacial / Rainy). Whole families included. Pick candidates, tag with #{tag} in-game, then ship via the Tagged-for-swap filter.
app.filter.cheap_evolve_hint: Cheap-XP grind: low-candy lines (Pidgey/Caterpie/Weedle/Wurmple/Whismur/Pidove) plus the pre-final members of every trade-evo family — but only when already traded (free post-trade evo).
app.filter.cheap_evolve_empty: Enable trade-evo protection or set an output locale where the early-game species names resolve.
app.filter.dex_plus_hint: Ready-to-evolve Pokémon that unlock a new dex entry. Excludes evolve-quest species (those need quest progress, not just candy).
app.filter.mega_evolve_hint: Mega rotation: every mega-eligible Pokémon not yet at max mega level. Mega-evolving them ranks each up — better catch bonuses, cheaper subsequent megas. Already at max are filtered out.
app.filter.pilot_long_hint: Catches ≥1000km from your home and not yet traded. The deeper Pilot-medal pile (the regular trade filter already covers ≥100km).
app.filter.raid_counter_hint: Pokémon whose own type resists {boss}'s STAB AND who carry a super-effective move type. Type filtering catches every form — Alolan Raichu shows up under Psychic, Mega Charizard X under Dragon. Pick your highest-level match; raid DPS is dominated by level + moveset, not IV.
app.filter.boss_no_clean_counter: {boss}: no clean counter — bring your highest-DPS attacker.
app.filter.aux_bosses_empty: No bosses in the current snapshot. Run `npm run fetch-raid-bosses` to refresh.
app.filter.aux_pvp_empty: No PvP rankings in the current snapshot. Run `npm run fetch-pvp-rankings` to refresh.
app.filter.aux_max_tank_empty: No charger moves in the current snapshot. Run `npm run fetch-meta-rankings` to refresh.
app.filter.max_tank_hint: Every Max-Battle-eligible Pokémon whose fast move is in the 0.5-second tier — these fill the Max Meter fastest (every fast-move tick generates 1 Max Energy regardless of damage, so faster ticks win). Layer your own type or CP filter on top in-game; pair with Blissey for healer duty.
app.filter.pvp_great_hint: Top-30 meta picks for Great League with loose PvP rank-1 IVs (atk 0-1, def 3-4, HP 3-4) under the 1500 CP cap. Family-search catches every form so an Alolan Raticate shows up under +raticate.
app.filter.pvp_ultra_hint: Top-30 meta picks for Ultra League with loose PvP rank-1 IVs under the 2500 CP cap. Some species cross over from Great League — they're in both lists when ranked highly in both.
app.filter.pvp_master_hint: Top-30 meta picks for Master League. Uncapped — species choice dominates IV perfection (a 3★ Zacian outperforms a hundo Metagross). No IV gate is applied; low attack is a handicap here, not a bait advantage. Review your roster's IVs in-game.
app.filter.aux_rocket_empty: No Rocket lineups in the current snapshot. Run `npm run fetch-rocket-lineups` to refresh.
app.filter.rocket_quote_lookup_title: Identify a grunt by their quote
app.filter.rocket_quote_lookup_hint: Even a partial quote works — fuzzy-matched against the in-game text in your output language.
app.filter.rocket_quote_no_match: No close match. Try a longer or cleaner quote.
app.filter.rocket_quote_match_generic: Generic grunt (#{n}) — lineup varies, no type clue.
app.clause_why.shadow_cheap_common: 1km buddy walk — common-rarity species (cheap stardust+candy to purify)
app.clause_why.frustration_move: still has the {move} legacy move (Charge TM target during take-over)
app.clause_why.frustration_unmoved: still in default state (has {move}) — skips shadows you've already Charge-TM'd
app.clause_why.evo_swap_candy_pool: expensive-evolution shadow species (whole family — base + evolved forms surface)
app.clause_why.evo_swap_not_traded: still tradeable as special trade (covers !lucky — lucky requires trade by definition)
app.clause_why.cheap_evolve_either: low-candy lines OR pre-final trade-evo members
app.clause_why.cheap_evolve_low_iv: skip 3★/4★ — don't waste high-IV on dex grind
app.clause_why.mega_progress_or_new: mega level 1-2 (room to rank up) OR never mega-evolved yet
app.clause_why.dex_plus_new_evo: evolution unlocks a new dex entry
app.clause_why.dex_plus_skip_quest: skip evolve-quest species (need quest progress, not just candy)
app.clause_why.raid_resistor_types: defender type resists the boss's STAB
app.clause_why.raid_se_fast: fast move type hits the boss super-effectively
app.clause_why.raid_se_charge: at least one charge move type hits the boss super-effectively
app.clause_why.raid_not_weak_to_boss: defender not weak to the boss's STAB attacks (applies to the allowlist too)
app.clause_why.rocket_not_weak_to_lineup: defender not weak to any STAB type that could appear in the lineup
app.clause_why.rocket_not_weak_to_common: defender not weak to STAB types that show up across most of the lineup (partial guard — rare lineup pulls may slip through)
app.clause_why.max_battle_dynamax_only: Dynamax-capable (only these can enter Max Battles)
app.clause_why.max_tank_chargers: fast move in the 0.5-second tier (fills the Max Meter fastest)
app.clause_why.rocket_resistor_types: defender type resists the Rocket lineup's STAB
app.clause_why.rocket_se_fast: fast move type hits at least one possible lineup Pokémon SE
app.clause_why.rocket_se_charge: at least one charge move type hits at least one possible lineup Pokémon SE
app.clause_why.rocket_top_offensive_fast: fast move in the top SE coverage across the lineup (best-effort — lineups vary)
app.clause_why.rocket_top_offensive_char: at least one charge move in the top SE coverage across the lineup
app.clause_why.pvp_meta_pool: top-30 meta family for the league
app.protect.shadow_keepers.count: {count} shadow keepers — top raid attackers worth keeping
app.protect.shadow_keepers.empty: no keepers — every shadow is fair game for the safe-purify filter
app.protect.shadow_keepers.input_placeho: Number or name — e.g. Tyranitar, Salamence, Metagross
app.protect.shadow_keepers.input_help: These shadow species (family-wide) are excluded from the safe-purify filter — the +20% atk shadow boost makes them top-tier raid attackers. Accepts {numbers}, {english}, or {german} names.
app.protect.raid_require_second_move.lab: Only show raid candidates with second charge move
app.protect.raid_require_second_move.hel: Adds `!@3move` to every per-boss filter, narrowing the result to attackers whose second charge move is already unlocked. Off by default so newer accounts still see candidates worth investing in.
app.preset.section_hint: Presets overwrite all settings below. Fine-tune from there.
app.preset.casual.label: Casual
app.preset.casual.description: All important protections on, lenient PvP
app.preset.collector.label: Collector
app.preset.collector.description: Recommended for dex completion — protects all forms
app.preset.aggressive.label: Aggressive
app.preset.aggressive.description: Fast cleanup — no form protection, strict PvP
app.preset.pvpFocus.label: PvP focus
app.preset.pvpFocus.description: Strict PvP IVs, protects league tags, otherwise aggressive
app.protect.section_title: Protections · what should never be tossed
app.protect.te_section_title: Trade-evo families · free evo via trade
app.protect.te_button_title: +{name} — when on, all family members (pre- and post-evolutions) are protected in the trade filter
app.protect.home_locals.note: These spawn locally for you — protecting them would be redundant and would shrink the trash filter for your hundos.
app.protect.myth_carve.empty: no carve-outs — all mythicals stay protected
app.protect.myth_carve.input_placeholder: Number or name — e.g. 808, Meltan, Genesect
app.protect.myth_carve.input_help: These are exempted from the mythical trash protection. Accepts {numbers}, {english}, or {german} names.
app.protect.favorites.label: Favorites
app.protect.any_tag.label: Tagged Pokémon (any tag)
app.protect.any_tag.why: any tag — anything tagged is safe
app.protect.trade_evos.label: Trade evolutions
app.protect.trade_evos.why: Protects families that get a free evolution by trading (Abra, Machop, Gastly, ...) — except those already traded (free evo already used)
app.protect.shinies.label: Shinies
app.protect.luckies.label: Lucky Pokémon
app.protect.legendaries.label: Legendaries
app.protect.legendaries.why: Mewtwo, Lugia, etc. — not bulk-tradeable anyway
app.protect.shadows.label: Shadow Pokémon
app.protect.shadows.why: Trash protection. Trade is blocked anyway (shadow are untradeable).
app.protect.costumes.label: Costumed
app.protect.costumes.why: Event forms like Pikachu with sunglasses
app.protect.backgrounds.label: With background
app.protect.legacy_moves.label: Legacy moves
app.protect.smeargle_legacy.label: Include Smeargle in legacy-move protection
app.protect.smeargle_legacy.why: Off (default): regular Smeargles get trashed. Every Smeargle has @special-flagged Sketched moves — without this carve-out the legacy-move rule would protect every single one. Flip on to revert and protect them all.
app.protect.babies.label: Baby Pokémon
app.protect.babies.why: Pokémon that only hatch from eggs
app.protect.xxl.label: XXL size
app.protect.xl.label: XL size
app.protect.xxs.label: XXS size
app.protect.new_evolutions.label: New evolutions
app.protect.new_evolutions.why: Pokémon whose evolution would be a new dex entry
app.protect.double_moved.label: Second charge move
app.protect.double_moved.why: Pokémon with the second charge move unlocked
app.protect.four_star.label: Protect 4★ hundos
app.protect.four_star.why: Safety net: never toss 4★ — Rule 1. DISABLE AT YOUR OWN RISK.
app.protect.mythicals.label: Mythicals
app.protect.ultra_beasts.label: Ultra Beasts
app.protect.purified.label: Purified
app.protect.purified.why: Former shadow Pokémon — purification cost stardust + candy
app.protect.dynamax.label: Dynamax-capable
app.protect.buddies_protect.label: Was a buddy
app.protect.buddies_protect.why: Sentimental protection — no one wants to trade away their old buddies
app.protect.lucky_eligible.label: Lucky-trade candidates
app.protect.lucky_eligible.why: Old untraded catches — likely guaranteed lucky trades. Adjust the cutoff year in safety nets below.
app.modal.settings.title: Settings
app.modal.language.output_help: Language of your PoGo app — determines what language the filter output uses
app.modal.language.output_mismatch: ⚠ Note: Filters output in {output} while the UI is in {ui}. Set both the same if your PoGo app matches your browser's language.
app.modal.settings.mode_normal_help: Sensible defaults — Expert for fine control
app.modal.tags.section_title: Trade tags · protected in the filter
app.modal.tags.frustration_help: manually flag any shadow for Charge-TM removal during take-over events
app.modal.tags.evo_swap_help: tag for trade-buddy purified-dex coordination — rename to run multiple parallel campaigns (e.g. EvoSwap-Maja)
app.modal.custom_tags.help: e.g. if you use PvPIV or PokeGenie
app.modal.league.help: embedded in the nickname — e.g. "ⓤ Rattata" for Ultra League
app.modal.safety.cp_hint: shows only Pokémon with CP ≤ value (default: 2000)
app.modal.safety.age_hint: shows only Pokémon from the last X days
app.modal.safety.lucky_year_hint: protects pre-20XX untraded catches (default: 21 → keeps 2020 and earlier; set 0 to disable)
app.modal.danger.reset_help: Resets the hundo list, all protections, tags, home location and trash list to defaults.
app.modal.backup.help: Round-trips your hundos, settings, buddies, home location and tags as JSON. Older exports are migrated automatically.
app.modal.backup.import_preview_dated: Found a workshop export from {date}. Will replace:
app.modal.backup.import_preview_undated: Found a workshop export. Will replace:
app.modal.backup.import_error_wrong_sche: This file isn't a workshop export.
app.modal.backup.import_error_unsupporte: Export was made with an unsupported schema version ({schema}).
app.buddy.section_title: Trade buddies · one tag prefix per buddy (e.g. #Auri)
app.buddy.section_help: In the game you'd tag things like {tag1} or {tag2}. PoGo's substring match protects all sub-tags automatically.
app.buddy_events.section_help: Which species are you currently collecting for your buddies? Surfaces in the "{filter_name}" filter — shows only trashable (0–2★) Pokémon of those species that you haven't tagged yet.
app.buddy_targets.te_toggle_title: Adds trade-evo families (Abra, Machop, Gastly, ...) to the catch filter — useful when the buddy collects free evolutions
app.buddy_targets.input_placeholder: Species (number or name) — e.g. Pikachu, 132, melmetal
app.regional_editor.home_local_title: automatically disabled — this species spawns locally for you (home)
app.regional.alolan.label: Alolan forms
app.regional.alolan.notes.raichu_psychic: Alolan Raichu (Electric/Psychic) — regular Raichu isn't Psychic
app.regional.galarian.label: Galarian forms
app.regional.hisuian.label: Hisuian forms
app.regional.hisuian.description: Pokémon Legends: Arceus — Hisuian regional forms
app.regional.paldean.label: Paldean forms
app.regional.regionals.label: Regional Pokémon
app.regional.collectibles.label: Collectibles
app.regional.collectibles.description: Multi-form / pattern collections — forms aren't separately searchable in PoGo
app.clause_why.trade_evo_family: Trade-evo: +{name} family (except already traded — free evo already used)
app.clause_why.buddy_tag: Trade buddy {name}: anything with the #{prefix} prefix (substring match)
app.clause_why.mythicals_carved: Mythicals except dex {carve} (you have spares of those)
app.clause_why.new_evolutions: New evolutions (trick: only if not yet mega'd — otherwise you have plenty of mega energy)
app.clause_why.double_moved_trash: Second charge move unlocked (@3move is inverted!)
app.clause_why.age_traded: Safety net: caught within {days} days OR traded OR purified
app.clause_why.lucky_eligible: Lucky-trade safety: pre-20{year} untraded catches protected (likely guaranteed lucky)
app.clause_why.must_traded: MUST: already-traded Pokémon can't be traded again
app.clause_why.must_shadow: MUST: Shadow Pokémon can't be traded
app.clause_why.must_lucky_long: MUST: Lucky Pokémon can't be traded again (already came from a trade)
app.clause_why.must_mythical_long: MUST: Mythicals untradeable (except Meltan/Melmetal via special trade)
app.clause_why.any_tag_trade: any tag — whichever, not for bulk trade
app.clause_why.bazaar_tag_trade: your bulk-trade tag #{tag} (only shows what you haven't yet marked — traded-list management)
app.clause_why.age_only: Safety net: ≤{days} days old OR purified
app.clause_why.distance_zero: Safety net: distance > 0km (= was caught somewhere else)
app.clause_why.must_lucky_short: MUST: Lucky can't be traded again
app.clause_why.trashable_stars: only trashable stars (0-2★) — you keep 3★+ yourself
app.clause_why.not_tagged: not already tagged in any way
app.clause_why.valuables_no_locals: Valuables: shiny, legendary, ultra beast, costume, background
app.clause_why.valuables_with_locals: Valuables: shiny, legendary, ultra beast, costume, background + local regionals ({count})
app.clause_why.untagged_or_marked: untagged OR marked for trade ({tags}) — other tags protected
app.map.local_already_title: already at home — friends get those via you
app.map.hint_with_home: hover the map to preview, click to pin
app.map.hint_no_home: hover the map, click your location, then "Set as home"
app.map.already_have_title: You catch these anyway — tag only if you want to mark them too
app.map.bazaar_empty_help: Tap the map to find regional Pokémon, then mark them with your trade tag {tag} in PoGo. The trash filter protects them automatically.
app.map.attribution: regional polygon data: u/zoglandboy / u/Mattman243 / pokemoncalendar.com
app.pvp.section_title: PvP mode · what counts as a keeper
app.pvp.help_loose: Loose keeps more Pokémon out of trash — every atk-IV 0 OR 1 spread with high defense/HP is protected (PvP rank-1 candidates). Wider safety net.
app.pvp.help_strict: Strict keeps only attack-IV 0 spreads with high defense/HP — classic PvP rank-1 only. Tighter trash, fewer false-positive keepers.
app.pvp.help_none: PvP carve-out disabled. The trash filter ignores low-attack PvP spreads entirely.
app.set_theory.rule1_help: Rule 1 (never toss 4★) is {auto} — any 4★ hundo trivially matches K, so ¬K excludes them. K and P are disjoint (atk≥3 vs atk≤1), so they don't overlap.
app.landing.title: Storage triage,
app.landing.subtitle: Your bag is full of fourteen kinds of special. This tool sorts them so you don't have to.
app.landing.problem.heading: The problem
app.landing.problem.body: Pokémon GO storage fills fast. Tossing the wrong one — a hundo, a shiny, a costume, a trade evolution — is unrecoverable. Most search strings handle two or three concerns. Yours probably needs to handle a dozen at once.
app.landing.idea.heading: The idea
app.landing.idea.body: Don't write down what to throw away. Write down what to keep. Then trash is everything else.
app.landing.idea.aside: It sounds backwards. It's much easier.
app.landing.complicated.heading: Why it gets messy
app.landing.complicated.body: Each of these is a separate keep-rule. Combine all of them and you've got a filter. Miss one and you toss something irreplaceable.
app.landing.complicated.items: Favorites • Shinies • Lucky friends • Hundos (and their evolutions) • Trade evolutions waiting for a swap • Costumed Pikachu • Event-background commemoratives • Pokémon with legacy moves • XXL / XL / XXS sizes • Shadows and purified • Mythicals you're hoarding • Buddies
app.landing.rule1.heading: Rule 1: 4★ are sacred
app.landing.rule1.body: No matter how the math comes out, a perfect IV Pokémon is never thrown away. Every other rule answers to this one.
app.landing.story.intro: Naive filters break the moment storage actually fills up. Here's the path from "keep what looks good" to a filter that survives a serious collection.
app.landing.story.act1.heading: 1. Keep the high-IV ones
app.landing.story.act1.body: Drop 0★, 1★, 2★. Keep 3★ and 4★. It's how every starter filter begins. It's also where every starter filter ends — and stops working.
app.landing.story.act2.heading: 2. …except the shinies and legendaries
app.landing.story.act2.body: Some low-IV Pokémon are irreplaceable. Shinies, legendaries, mythicals, ultra beasts — they cost events or coins to replace. Carve them out of the trash bucket regardless of IV.
app.landing.story.act3.heading: 3. Tagged for friends, favorited for you
app.landing.story.act3.body: You tagged a Bagon for the friend who needs one. You favorited that 1★ Pikachu from a memorable raid. The filter respects your tags and favorites across every IV tier.
app.landing.story.act4.heading: 4. PvP is a low-IV game
app.landing.story.act4.body: Battle League rewards low attack with high defense and HP. The contenders mostly live in the 1★/2★ buckets you were about to trash. Pull them back.
app.landing.story.act5.heading: 5. The hundo unlock
app.landing.story.act5.body: Storage is full of 3★ you'll never actually use. Once you have a hundo of that family, the regular 3★s are surplus — loosen the keep on those, but spare the ones with two stats at 15 (the K rule), because then we don't feel like we throw away everything!
app.landing.finisher.heading: And the result?
app.landing.finisher.lead: Look at all the beautiful space we have for more
app.landing.regional.intro_body: Pokémon GO has regional Pokémon — Tauros in North America, Mr. Mime in Europe, Heracross in South America. The filter knows where you live, where you've been, and what to do about both.
app.landing.regional.act1_body: Travel and you catch regionals you normally can't reach. The workshop treats every regional as a collectible by default, so your Mr. Mime from Paris and your Heracross from São Paulo all stay protected — at least until you settle somewhere their species lives.
app.landing.regional.act2_body: Set your home location and the workshop figures out which regionals walk past your door. Those species auto-drop from collector protection — you'll catch more next week. Anything you favorited or tagged keeps its protection across the transition (favorites are protected regardless of region).
app.landing.trade.intro_body: Your storage isn't only yours. Friends want low-IV starters for free evolutions, hundo seekers want lucky-trade fuel, distant catches cost less stardust to swap. The trade filter mirrors trash — same engine, different keeper set.
app.landing.trade.act1_body: Pokémon caught 100km or more from your friend qualify as long-distance trades — the only thing that fills the Pilot medal. The workshop protects them as a separate stash so the only proof you went somewhere doesn't end up in the bin. Niantic added an in-game Fern-Tausch tag in late 2025 for exactly this; the filter respects it.
app.landing.trade.act2_body: Kadabra, Machoke, Graveler, Haunter, Boldore, and friends evolve for free when traded. Tradition: keep the lowest-IV candidate per family — the one you'd normally trash is exactly what your friend wants for their dex entry.
app.landing.trade.act2_caption: What you'd trash → what they want
app.landing.trade.act3_body: Add a friend's name in Settings → Trade buddies and you get a personal tag (#auri, #julia). Tag a Bagon "#auri" and the filter stages it for Auri automatically. Pre-tag during the long climb up storage; trade when you next meet up.
app.landing.trade.act3_caption: Each buddy gets their own stash
app.rules.title: The rules,
app.rules.intro: Every protection in the workshop earns its place by answering one question: would tossing this hurt? Here's the catalogue, grouped by why we keep things.
app.rules.group.safety.heading: Safety nets
app.rules.group.rarity.heading: Rarity protection
app.rules.group.rarity.note: If it cost an event or a coin, it stays.
app.rules.group.cosmetic.heading: Cosmetic & historical
app.rules.group.smart.heading: Smart logic
app.rules.group.smart.note: Where the workshop earns its name.
app.rules.group.battle.heading: Battle league
app.rules.r1.title: Never toss a 4★
app.rules.r1.body: Perfect IVs (15/15/15) cost a thousand catches and a lot of luck. The filter never lets you trash one — even if every other rule disagrees. This rule overrules everything else.
app.rules.r2.title: Never toss a favorite
app.rules.r2.body: If you favorited it, you had a reason. The filter respects favorites unconditionally — the toggle is on by default in normal mode and only expert mode lets you turn it off.
app.rules.r3.title: Shinies are forever
app.rules.r3.body: Even a 0★ shiny took thousands of taps or an event window to find. Shinies are protected at every IV tier — they never appear in the trash filter.
app.rules.r4.title: Legendaries, mythicals, ultra beasts
app.rules.r4.body: Raid-or-research-only species are protected by default. Mythicals have a carve-out for spares: you can declare "too many of" species (e.g. Meltan, Genesect) so the surplus becomes tradeable.
app.rules.r5.title: Lucky friends
app.rules.r5.body: Lucky Pokémon cost half the stardust to power up — even a meh-IV lucky beats a hundo of the same species in some matchups. Always protected.
app.rules.r6.title: Costumed Pokémon
app.rules.r6.body: Party hat Pikachu, flower-crown Eevee, Halloween Gengar — all event-only and irreplaceable. Protected.
app.rules.r7.title: Event backgrounds
app.rules.r7.body: Pokémon caught during certain events get a permanent commemorative background — Pokémon Day, GO Fest, Halloween, and so on. They're impossible to recreate, so the workshop protects them as date-stamped souvenirs.
app.rules.r8.title: Legacy moves
app.rules.r8.body: Some Pokémon were taught moves only available during specific Community Days. Once gone, gone. Anything carrying a legacy move is locked in — except Smeargle, whose Sketched moveset is permanently flagged @special on every catch. The workshop carves Smeargle out of this rule by default; flip 'Include Smeargle in legacy-move protection' in expert settings to revert.
app.rules.r9.title: Sizes (XXL / XL / XXS)
app.rules.r9.body: Size collectors care; the medal hunt is real. Each size tier has its own toggle and protects accordingly.
app.rules.r10.title: Shadows protected, never traded
app.rules.r10.body: Shadow Pokémon get a 20% attack boost — they're raid powerhouses. Team GO Rocket take-over events and trainer level-up tasks regularly ask you to purify shadows, so the workshop keeps them safe by default. The trade filter excludes them anyway: shadows are untradeable in PoGo.
app.rules.r11.title: Tagged Pokémon survive everything
app.rules.r11.body: Anything carrying any in-game tag (custom or default) is protected. Mark a Bagon "#trade" for a friend — the workshop will never recommend tossing it.
app.rules.r12.title: Trade-evolution candidates
app.rules.r12.body: Kadabra, Machoke, Graveler, Haunter, and other trade-evo lines evolve for free when traded. The filter holds onto the lowest-IV candidate per family so trade buddies don't go empty-handed.
app.rules.r13.title: The hundo unlock
app.rules.r13.body: Once your collection contains a 4★ of a family, regular 3★s of that family lose their generic keep — except the ones with two stats at 15 and the third at 11 or above (the workshop's actual K rule), kept because they're still PvP-relevant and tossing every 3★ feels harsh.
app.rules.r14.title: Home location → regional auto-drop
app.rules.r14.body: Set your home location and the filter auto-removes locals from collector-style protections (you're catching them weekly, you don't need to hoard). Trip catches you specifically want to keep across the move? Favorite or tag them — those rules hold regardless of region.
app.rules.r15.title: PvP loose mode
app.rules.r15.body: Battle League rewards low atk + high def + high HP. Loose mode carves these out of the trash bucket across IV tiers; strict mode tightens to specific stat product floors. Off if you don't play GBL.
app.landing.nerd.intro: The whole thing is a tiny set algebra. Five-second tour:
app.landing.nerd.k_def: K — the keeper set. Union of every "protect" toggle: favorites, shinies, hundos, trade evos, regionals, costumes, legacy moves, sizes, shadows, …
app.landing.nerd.t_def: T = ¬K — the trash filter. Every Pokémon not in any keeper set.
app.landing.nerd.species_def: H ∪ S012 — the species clause. Your hundo families, expanded through evolution lines, plus 0/1/2★ wildcards.
app.landing.nerd.intersection_def: Clauses are joined with & — set intersection. Every clause must hold for a Pokémon to land in the result.
app.landing.nerd.symmetry: The trade filter is the same engine, different K. Define what you'd give away; the rest stays.
app.landing.nerd.rule1: Rule 1 falls out of the math: 4★ Pokémon trivially match K (any keeper rule that mentions IVs catches them), so ¬K never picks them. The safety net is structural, not bolted on.
app.landing.demo.label: Watch a rule sweep through:
app.algebra.title: Set algebra,
app.algebra.intro: PoGo's search box was built for finding Pokémon, not for throwing them away. The workshop's job is to turn 'I want to clear my storage' into a string the search box can actually use. The path goes through set theory.
app.algebra.s1.heading: 1. The search box only knows how to show
app.algebra.s1.body: Pokémon GO gives you one input field and a tiny grammar. Type and the box shows whatever your terms describe — combined, intersected, or excluded by these operators:
app.algebra.s1.precedence_body: OR binds tighter than AND in PoGo — the inverse of most languages. Combined with no parens, this forces every expression to land in AND-of-ORs form (conjunctive normal form, CNF). Section 8 has the workout.
app.algebra.s2.heading: 2. The inversion
app.algebra.s2.body: Here's the friction: search results are what you'd see in a list. But you don't want a list of trash — you want to bulk-select what's in your trash bin and tap delete. So the search string has to describe what BELONGS in the bin: 'show me everything that isn't worth keeping.'
app.algebra.s2.naive1_body: 0*,1*,2* — but that trashes your low-IV shiny Mr. Mime, your tagged trade-evo Kadabra, and the 1★ Pikachu from a memorable raid.
app.algebra.s2.naive2_body: Explodes combinatorially the moment you have 100 species and 10 keep rules per species. The string would blow past PoGo's 5,000-character limit before you got to Charizard.
app.algebra.s2.working_body: Describe what's NOT covered by any keep rule. Now adding a keep rule shrinks the trash by exactly the right amount — overlaps and edge cases handle themselves.
app.algebra.s3.heading: 3. Set algebra to the rescue
app.algebra.s3.body: Lift each protection out of natural language and into a set. Each keep rule is a subset of your storage. Union them all and you've got the keepers; the complement is the trash.
app.algebra.s3.def_u_value: your storage — every Pokémon you currently own
app.algebra.s3.def_ki_value: the i-th keep rule (favorites, shinies, hundos, …) — each is a subset of U
app.algebra.s3.def_k_value: the keepers — union of every keep rule
app.algebra.s3.def_t_value: the trash candidates — everything in U that isn't in K
app.algebra.s4.heading: 4. De Morgan's bridge
app.algebra.s4.body: The set definition tells you what trash is. The search syntax doesn't speak unions. To get from one to the other, lean on De Morgan's laws — the rule that says 'NOT (A OR B)' is the same as 'NOT A AND NOT B'. The second form maps directly onto the search box: '!' is NOT, '&' is AND.
app.algebra.s5.heading: 5. Worked example
app.algebra.s5.body: Three keep rules. Watch them turn into one filter string.
app.algebra.s5.result_label: Result — paste into the search box:
app.algebra.s5.result_note: The actual workshop builds 10–20 more clauses for trade evos, regionals, sizes, costumes, legacy moves, etc. Same machinery, more terms, all stitched together with the same '&'.
app.algebra.s6.heading: 6. K, visualised
app.algebra.s6.body: Three of the workshop's keep sets, drawn properly. Sample Pokémon dots populate the regions — including the intersections where multiple rules apply. Anything outside K is in T, pulsing red — those are the trash candidates the filter is asking the search box to show.
app.algebra.s7.heading: 7. Trade is symmetric
app.algebra.s7.body: The trade filter uses the same engine, swapped out for a different keeper set: 'things you'd send to a friend.' Different K_trade, same De Morgan dance, same shape of search string. Once the inversion is solved, every other filter in the workshop is a free re-skin.
app.algebra.s8.heading: 8. The parens PoGo doesn't have
app.algebra.s8.body: PoGo's syntax has no parentheses AND it binds OR tighter than AND — the inverse of most languages. The combined effect: every filter has to land in AND-of-ORs form (conjunctive normal form, CNF). Set theory's distributive law A ∪ (B ∩ C) = (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) is the move that gets you there.
app.algebra.s8.ex1_title: Trash the Pikachu spares — keep Alolan Raichu
app.algebra.s8.ex1_intent: Got a Pikachu hundo. Now even the 3★ Pikachu are surplus — but never the Alolan Raichu, that's a regional you can't re-catch in your home zone.
app.algebra.s8.ex1_typetrick_note: PoGo does have region keywords (`alola`, `hisui`, `paldea`), so `alola&raichu` is a valid way to address Alolan Raichu directly. The type substitution is just more compact and composes better in CNF: Alolan Raichu is Electric/Psychic while regular Raichu is just Electric, so `!psychic` cleanly excludes the Alolan form without dragging an extra clause around. The workshop's raid / max-battle / rocket counter filters use the same trick — typing `psychic` catches every Psychic-typed Pokémon, regional variants included.
app.algebra.s8.ex2_intent: Pidgey costs 12 candy to evolve. A traded Geodude carries the trade flag through evolution — 25 candy to Graveler, then Golem evolves for free. Both species are 'cheap full evolves' worth keeping for medal/lucky farming.
app.algebra.s8.ex2_note: Precedence trap: with , binding tighter than &, the naive `pidgey,geodude&traded` parses as `(pidgey ∪ geodude) ∩ traded` — find me anything that's pidgey or geodude AND was traded. That requires the Pidgey to be traded too, missing the whole point. Distribution to CNF gives the right thing.
app.algebra.s8.laws_label: Distributive laws — the move that flattens to CNF
app.algebra.s8.laws_intro: The first identity is the workhorse: it converts any OR-of-ANDs (DNF) into AND-of-ORs (CNF), the only form PoGo's syntax can parse. The second is its dual — going the other way produces DNF, which can't be pasted.
app.algebra.s9.heading: 9. The whole trick, in three identities
app.algebra.s9.body: Pull eight sections of math into the smallest possible package: three identities are all the algebra you need. Everything the workshop does for you compiles to combinations of these.
app.algebra.s9.move1_caption: Describe what stays, take the complement. Trash falls out for free.
app.algebra.s9.move2_caption: A union of keepers becomes an intersection of negations. The clean form for the trash filter.
app.algebra.s9.move3_caption: Any nested AND/OR flattens into AND-of-ORs. The only shape PoGo's parser accepts.
app.algebra.s9.manifesto: Three identities. Five thousand characters of search box.
app.algebra.s9.manifesto_punch: Ask it anything you can phrase as a set.
app.algebra.s9.manifesto_credit: And the nice part — the workshop runs the algebra for you, every click. You bring the intent.
